name: Chicken Spaghetti Casserole
recipeIngredient: Mushrooms (1/2 lb, chopped or 1 small can)
recipeInstructions:
1. Simmer chicken in water with soup greens until tender.
- A large, old hen may take up to two hours. The process can be done in a Crockpot, cooking all day.
2. Remove the cooked chicken from the stock, discarding the vegetables and reserving the stock. Stir the meat from bones, discard the skin and bones, and cut the meat into small pieces.
3. In a large skillet, sauté the first five casserole ingredients (vegetables) in oil until just tender. Remove to a large mixing bowl and hold in reserve.
4. Add butter to the pan and make a roux with the flour. Cook over Low heat until the flour is cooked but does not brown.
5. Gradually add milk, stirring constantly until the sauce thickens.
6. Add the remaining mushrooms (or red peppers) to cool the sauce. Process in a food processor with a steel blade until very fine.
7. Add to the vegetables in the mixing bowl.
8. Combine chicken, pimientos or red peppers, and 1 cup of shredded cheese. Mix well.
9. Meanwhile, break vermicelli into small pieces (3-4 inches long) and cook in some of the chicken stock until just al dente. Drain.
10. Combine cooked vermicelli with the prepared sauce.
11. Gently fold in the hard-boiled eggs.
12. Place the casserole in two greased flat oval dishes and bake at 300°F for about 45 minutes. If the casserole appears too dry, add some of the reserved chicken stock.
13. About 10 minutes before done, sprinkle the remaining cheese on top. The second casserole can be frozen.
